what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement — a "Host:" header making ptr non-NULL — but reached purely by extra havoc/token mutation budget rather than CMP introspection, since both fast and minimizer lack I2S.
why_winner_satisfies: fast resolves 8/10 vs minimizer 2/10 with hitcount 394 vs 75; fast's AFLFast FAST power schedule gives rarely-hit coverage buckets up to 4x energy, so the rare seeds that already contain a partial "Host:" header get many more havoc mutations and more often complete the literal.
why_loser_doesnt: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None with energy from intrinsic calibration only, not weighted by region rarity, so the rare path to the "Host:"-bearing input gets no extra mutation budget and it blocks 8/10 with far lower hitcount (75).
mechanism_attribution: The fast vs minimizer delta is purely the AFLFast `n_fuzz`-based rare-edge power schedule (FAST) which multiplies the mutation budget for rarely-exercised coverage buckets; neither fuzzer has CMP logging, so the win is extra havoc dosing on rare paths, not comparison-solving.

what_input_feature: Same structural Host-header / cookie-engine input as above, but reached without CMP introspection — purely via more havoc energy budgeted to the rarely-hit seed that approaches the cookie path.
why_winner_satisfies: fast uses the AFLFast FAST power schedule giving rarely-hit coverage buckets up to 4x energy, so the seed near the cookie branch receives more havoc mutations and resolves 8/10 with avg hitcount 214 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None (intrinsic calibration energy only, not weighted by region rarity), so the same rare path gets ordinary budget and resolves only 2/10 with avg hitcount 53.
mechanism_attribution: fast's AFLFast rare-edge power schedule (n_fuzz-based perf_score boost) concentrates havoc budget on the rarely-exercised cookie path; minimizer's calibration-only energy does not, so fast wins by scheduling not by CMP-solving.
